titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gail and Stephen Rudin American presidential collection, 1775-2009.
Rudin, Gail and Stephen American presidential collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Donor

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

profiledesc.creation: Finding aid encoded by RMC StaffEvan Earle, October 2009

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the Gail And Stephen Rudin American Presidential Collection, 1775-2009.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.unittitle: Gail and Stephen Rudin American presidential collection,
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Historical letters, documents and ephemera relating to American presidential campaigns from Abraham Lincoln to Barack Obama.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Historical letters, documents and ephemera relating to American presidential campaigns from Abraham Lincoln to Barack Obama. Newspapers include issues of the New York Times from Nov. 23-26, 1963, focusing on the assassination of President Kennedy and its aftermath.

did.unittitle: Series I. Abraham Lincoln
Documents
Lincoln, Abraham Office held: As U.S. Representative from Illinois

scopecontent.p: Lincoln's patent to lift boats over shoals, written while Lincoln served as a representative in the Illinois legislature. See separated materials below. Rudin # L-1

did.unittitle: Lincoln, Abraham Office held: As President Writer's location: Washington D.C.

scopecontent.p: General Orders No. 141: Suspension of Habeas Corpus. Rudin # L-1C

did.unittitle: Lincoln, Abraham Office held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington D.C.

scopecontent.p: General Orders No. 89: Dishonorable dismissal of Lt. J.M. Garland. Rudin # L-1B

did.unittitle: Lincoln, Abraham Office held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington D.C.

scopecontent.p: General Orders No. 252: Order threatening the execution of prisoners. Rudin # L-1D

did.unittitle: Meade, Gen. George and Ruggles, Gen. George Writer's location: Headquarters of the Army of the Potomoc

scopecontent.p: General Orders No. 15 and No. 16: Announcements of Lincoln's death and the details of official mourning ceremonies. Rudin # L-1A

did.unittitle: Letters, Envelopes, and Postcards
Envelope

scopecontent.p: Envelope advertising the Lincoln-Hamlin campaign of 1860, depicting beardless Lincoln splitting rails. Rudin # L-4A

did.unittitle: Parks, Samuel C. Writer's location: Lincoln, IllinoisAddressee: Robert B. Lantham

scopecontent.p: Letter describing Lincoln's nighttime trip into Washington to avoid assassination after his election. Sent from Lincoln, Ill. Rudin # L-2B

did.unittitle: "William"Writer's location: Metropolitan Hotel, Washington D.C. Addressee: My dear Mother

scopecontent.p: Letter describing a visit with Lincoln in the Oval Office. Rudin # L-2

did.unittitle: Envelope

scopecontent.p: Envelope advertising the Lincoln-Johnson campaign of 1864, with jugate images of candidates in profile. Rudin # L-4B

did.unittitle: Envelopes

scopecontent.p: Series of envelopes with political cartoons of Lincoln and Jefferson Davis fighting a boxing match. Rudin # L-4E

did.unittitle: Postcards

scopecontent.p: Postcards depicting the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C. Rudin # L-5

did.unittitle: Postcards

scopecontent.p: Postcards related to the Gettysburg Address. Rudin # L-5A

did.unittitle: Envelope

scopecontent.p: Civil War envelope, depicting a hand-colored image of beardless Lincoln. Rudin # L-4C

did.unittitle: Envelope

scopecontent.p: Envelope for 1860 campaign, depicting beardless Lincoln and topped with a liberty cap between flags. Rudin # L-4D

did.unittitle: Leaming, James Writer's location: Bridgeton, N.J. Addressee: Reuben Learning (writer's brother)

scopecontent.p: Letter discussing the death of Lincoln and what it means to the country. Sent from Bridgeton, N.J. Rudin # L-2C

did.unittitle:
"James"
Addressee: My dear Wife

scopecontent.p: Letters giving first-hand accounts of events just after Lincoln's assassination. Rudin # L-2A

did.unittitle: Envelope Writer's location: Cincinnati, OHAddressee: James G. Barnwell

scopecontent.p: Envelope printed with mourning cover and portrait in honor of Lincoln's death. Rudin # L-4

did.unittitle: Envelope

scopecontent.p: 5 Envelopes commemorating the 100th and 150th anniversary of the Emancipation Proclamation, with stamps

did.unittitle: Envelope

scopecontent.p: Envelope commemorating 85th anniversary of the Gettysburg Address, with stamp

did.unittitle: Postcards

scopecontent.p: Postcards related to Lincoln's assassination. Rudin # L-5B

did.unittitle: Postcards

scopecontent.p: Postcards depicting busts, statues, and monuments of Lincoln. Rudin # L-5C

did.unittitle: 96 postcards

scopecontent.p: Postcards of Lincoln, Mary Lincoln, and various scenes.

did.unittitle: Ribbons, Badges, Medals, and Tokens
Ribbon

scopecontent.p: Ribbon advertising the 1860 Lincoln-Hamlin campaign. Rudin # L-6

did.unittitle: Token and ribbon

scopecontent.p: Artifacts promoting Lincoln's 1864 campaign. Rudin # L-6A

did.unittitle: Badge

scopecontent.p: Badge with Lincoln's portrait promoting his 1864 campaign. Rudin # L-6B

did.unittitle: Ribbon

scopecontent.p: Ribbon worn after Lincoln's assassination. Rudin # L-6C

did.unittitle: Ribbon

scopecontent.p: Mourning ribbon reading, "In Memoriam - Our Country's Martyr," worn after Lincoln's assassination. Rudin # L-6D

did.unittitle: Badge

scopecontent.p: Mourning ribbon reading, "In Memory of Abraham Lincoln - Died April 15, 1865." Rudin # L-6E

did.unittitle: Ribbon

scopecontent.p: Mourning ribbon reading, "True in Life, True in Death - Our Country Mourns the Loss of her Best Son." Rudin # L-6F

did.unittitle: Medal

scopecontent.p: Medal advertising the Lincoln-Johnson campaign of 1864, with jugate images of candidates in profile. Rudin # L-7

did.unittitle: Tokens

scopecontent.p: Campaign tokens advertising Lincoln's 1860 candidacy. Rudin # L-7A

did.unittitle: Two key chains

scopecontent.p: Two bicentennial memorial key chains.

did.unittitle: Songs
Song sheet

scopecontent.p: Song sheet printed with lyrics to "Abe Lincoln's Union Wagon," by C. Magnus. Rudin # L-8

did.unittitle: Songbook

scopecontent.p: Campaign songbook entitled, "The Wide-Awake Vocalist; or, Rail Splitter's Song Book." Rudin # L-8C

did.unittitle: Song sheet

scopecontent.p: Song sheet printed with lyrics to "The Nation Mourns." Rudin # L-8A

did.unittitle: Song sheet

scopecontent.p: Song sheet printed with lyrics to "President Lincoln's Funeral March." Rudin # L-8B

did.unittitle: Song sheet

scopecontent.p: Song sheet printed with lyrics to "Rest Noble Chieftain - Song on the Death of Abraham Lincoln." Rudin # L-8D

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Objects
Coins
did.physdesc: One penny in plastic enclosure; canvas bag of assorted other pennies.

scopecontent.p: Lincoln penny from 1909 with sculptor's initials, V.D.B., plus a one-pound bag of assorted Lincoln pennies. Rudin # L-9

did.unittitle: Ballot

scopecontent.p: Ballot from the 1860 election. Rudin # L-10

did.unittitle: Ballot

scopecontent.p: Ballot from the 1864 election. Rudin # L-10A

did.unittitle: Carte de Visite

scopecontent.p: Visiting card with Matthew Brady's photograph of Lincoln later used for the image on the penny. Rudin # L-10J

did.unittitle: Invitation

scopecontent.p: Invitation to the Lincoln-Johnson inaugural ball. Rudin # L-10B

did.unittitle: Order of Funeral Procession

scopecontent.p: Booklet describing the order and customs for Lincoln's funeral procession. Rudin # L-10F

did.unittitle: Carte de Visite

scopecontent.p: Visiting card depicting Lincoln's funeral procession in Philadelphia. Rudin # L-10G

did.unittitle: Stationary

scopecontent.p: Stationary with tinted letterhead depicting Lincoln's funeral procession in New York. Rudin # L-10H

did.unittitle: Stereoview

scopecontent.p: Stereoview of Ford's Theater in Washington, D.C. Rudin # L-10D

did.unittitle: Stereoview

scopecontent.p: Stereoview of the Customs House in Boston, Mass., displaying mourning drape after Lincoln's death. Rudin # L- Rudin # L-10E

did.unittitle: Dish

scopecontent.p: A milk glass souvenir dish with a split-rail pattern. Rudin # L-10K

did.unittitle: Bust

scopecontent.p: A profile bust of Lincoln made from macerated pennies. Rudin # L-10L

did.unittitle: Relief portrait

scopecontent.p: A relief portrait of Lincoln's profile, set in its original case. Rudin # L-10M

did.unittitle: Tile

scopecontent.p: A framed ceramic pottery tile depicting an image of Lincoln. Rudin # L-10N

did.unittitle: Engraving

scopecontent.p: A framed wooden engraving of Lincoln's image. Rudin # L-10P

did.unittitle: Gravy boat

scopecontent.p: A Staffordshire gravy boat depicting Lincoln delivering the Gettysburg Address. Rudin # L-10Q

did.unittitle: Broadside

scopecontent.p: Broadside from Ford's Theater, advertising the show the night Lincoln was shot. Rudin # L-10C

did.unittitle: Lithograph

scopecontent.p: Hand-colored lithograph depicting the Lincoln Memorial.

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: 200th anniversary commemorative stamp and coin set.

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: Etching with 1-cent and 3-cent stamp, 4-cent printed stamp on note card, 2008 commemorative stamp folio sheets, 200th anniversary first day of issue stamps.

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: 1954 envelope with 4-cent stamps, 1940 13th amendment stamp, four 1909 2-cent stamps.

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: Commemorative envelopes with sheets of 4-cent, 3-cent, and 1-cent stamps

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: Commemorative envelopes with 4-cent, 5-cent, and 25-cent airmail stamps.

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: Various Lincoln stamps from St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Marshall Islands, Gabon, Rwanda, Sao Tome and Principe, Togo, Liberia, Guinea-Bissau, Democratic Republic of Congo, and 1989 World Stamp Expo.

did.unittitle: Stamp and envelope

scopecontent.p: Postal-used Lincoln patriotic cover with three-cent stamp.

did.unittitle: Stamps

scopecontent.p: Forever stamps, in sheets, commemorating 150th anniversary of the Emancipation Proclamation. First Day of Issue, with USPS envelope. Rudin # L-4A

did.unittitle: Stamps, poster

scopecontent.p: Forever Stamp sheet, with original print by the designer

did.unittitle: Newspapers
Harper's Weekly

scopecontent.p: Coverage of Lincoln's announcement that he will become a presidential candidate. Rudin # L-3H

did.unittitle: New York Tribune

scopecontent.p: Coverage of the South's secession from the Union.

did.unittitle: Harper's Weekly

scopecontent.p: Coverage of the Emancipation Proclamation. Rudin # L-3E

did.unittitle: National Anti-Slavery Standard

scopecontent.p: Coverage of compensated emancipation. Rudin # L-3K

did.unittitle: The New York Times

scopecontent.p: Coverage of the Emancipation Proclamation. Rudin # L-3F

did.unittitle: The Philadelphia Inquirer

scopecontent.p: Coverage of the Emancipation Proclamation and slavery. Rudin # L-3J

did.unittitle: New York Tribune

scopecontent.p: Coverage of Lincoln's amnesty proclamation. Rudin # L-3I

did.unittitle: The New York Times

scopecontent.p: Coverage of passage of the 13th Amendment. Rudin # L-3G

did.unittitle: The New York Herald

scopecontent.p: Reprint of Lincoln assassination coverage. Rudin # L-3

did.unittitle: The New York Times

scopecontent.p: Lincoln assassination coverage. Rudin # L-3A

did.unittitle: The New York Times

scopecontent.p: Lincoln assassination coverage. Rudin # L-3B

did.unittitle: Harper's Weekly

scopecontent.p: Lincoln assassination coverage. Rudin # L-3D

did.unittitle: Series II. Other U.S. presidents
American President stamps

scopecontent.p: Binder, "The Presidential First Day Cover Collection"

did.unittitle: American Presidents

scopecontent.p: Foreign stamp sheets of U.S. presidents.
Envelope with 4 6-cent Adams stamps, note card with stamped 6-cent stamp, 4 6-cent stamps.

did.unittitle: Adams, John Office held: As member of Massachusetts Governor's Council

scopecontent.p: Original 10/11/1775, correction 8/16/1776. Appointing Justices of the Inferior Court of Common Pleas. All references to King George III and Great Britain are crossed out. Rudin # P-1

did.unittitle: Adams, John QuincyOffice held: While representing Massachusetts in the House of RepresentativesAddressee: Solomon Lincoln, Josiah Brigham, Joseph RichardsWriter's location: Quincy, MA

scopecontent.p: Conduct in campaigning for the Presidency. Rudin # P-2

did.unittitle: Arthur, ChesterOffice held: Customs Collector for the Port of New YorkAddressee: Commander of the USS AlaskaWriter's location: New York

scopecontent.p: Smuggling of Wines, Cigars and Silks. Rudin # P-3

did.unittitle: Buchanan, JamesOffice held: As Secretary of StateWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Announces his appointment as Secretary of State by President Polk. Dated his first day in office. Rudin # P-4

did.unittitle: Buchanan, JamesOffice held: As Secretary of StateAddressee: John WadmanWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Seeking the 1848 nomination for President. Rudin # P-5

did.unittitle: Buchanan, JamesOffice held: No elective office heldAddressee: John M. JonesWriter's location: Wheatland near Lancaster, PA

scopecontent.p: Supporting the wrong candidate for office might damage his chances for the 1852 Presidential nomination. Rudin # P-6

did.unittitle: Buchanan, JamesOffice held: No elective office heldAddressee: Barnabas Bates, Assistant Postmaster Writer's location: Wheatland near Lancaster, PA

scopecontent.p: After failing to gain the Democratic Presidential nomination. Rudin # P-7

did.unittitle: Buchanan, JamesOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: William L. Marcy, Secretary of StateWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: On his first full day as President. Rudin # P-8

did.unittitle: Bush, George, H.W.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: "Dr. Ray S. Cline - Chairman, U.S. Global Strategy Council"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Discusses our policy toward China. Rudin # P-9

did.unittitle: Bush, George, H.W.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: Giulio Andreotti, Prime Minister of ItalyWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: "I valued our substantive discussions - our transatlantic ties have never been better". Rudin # P-10

did.unittitle: Bush, George H.W.Office held: As PresidentWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Announcing the beginning of the 1991 war against Iraq. From the Forbes Collection - Part 3. Rudin # P-11
New York Times coverage of Iraq war.

did.unittitle: Bush, George, H.W.Office held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Ron WeekesWriter's location: Kennebunkport, ME

scopecontent.p: Describing his state of mind and the goal of the Presidency. Rudin # P-12

did.unittitle: Bush, George, H.W.Office held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Daniel Patrick Moynihan, SenatorWriter's location: Houston, TX

scopecontent.p: Discusses Most Favored Nation Trade Status for China. Rudin # P-13

did.unittitle: Bush, George H.W.Office held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Melville & Lizanne ChapinWriter's location: Houston, TX

scopecontent.p: On the gubernatorial elections of his two sons. Rudin # P-14

did.unittitle: Bush, George, H.W.Office held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Dear HelenWriter's location: Houston, TX

scopecontent.p: Not interested in writing a memoir, "Thanks for your support of George W. He's a good man and he won't let you down." Rudin # P-15

did.unittitle: Bush, George W. Office held: As Governor of TexasAddressee: Bill ChristiansenWriter's location: Austin, TX

scopecontent.p: "every Texan has a key role in making Texas a greater state". Rudin # P-16

did.unittitle: Bush, George W. Office held: As Governor of TexasAddressee: Ralph BensonWriter's location: Austin, TX

scopecontent.p: Concerning running for President. Rudin # P-17

did.unittitle: Bush, George W. Office held: As Governor of TexasAddressee: Bill ChristiansenWriter's location: Austin, TX

scopecontent.p: About the direction of our youth and the teaching of morals and values. Rudin # P-18

did.unittitle: Bush, George, W.Office held: As Governor of TexasAddressee: Chip FreundtWriter's location: Austin, TX

scopecontent.p: Describes his feelings for his father. Rudin # P-19
Two newspapers on 9/11 and the Bush administration.

did.unittitle: Bush, George, W.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: "Kevin C. Jenkins, Mayor Rhinelander, Wisconsin"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: "We can create an environment where small business flourishes and Americans... can find jobs". Rudin # P-20

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Bruce BeckerWriter's location: Plains, GA

scopecontent.p: Re: Religion. Rudin # P-21

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As Candidate for PresidentAddressee: Jimmy Carter

scopecontent.p: Notes from speech writer Pat Anderson. Rudin # P-22

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As Candidate for PresidentAddressee: Al Smith DinnerWriter's location: New York

scopecontent.p: "Two weeks before Election Day 1976." Draft of speech for Al Smith Dinner. Rudin # P-23

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As President-ElectAddressee: Congressman Del ClaussonWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Increasing the effectiveness of the Executive Branch. Rudin # P-24

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Robert P. Griffin, SenatorWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Encourages the sale of military aircraft to Israel, Egypt and Saudi Arabia. Rudin # P-25

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Excerpt from Carter's speech as the Camp David Peace Treaty was signed. Rudin # P-26

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Daniel Patrick Moynihan, SenatorWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Re: The Salt Treaty. Rudin # P-27
"Keeping Faith. Memoirs of a President" Bantam Books, New York, NY. Signed copy. See separated material below. Rudin # P-28

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As Ex PresidentWriter's location: New York

scopecontent.p: Contract for "Keeping Faith" between President Carter and Bantam Books. Rudin # P-29

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Robert Garcia, CongressmanWriter's location: Atlanta, GA

scopecontent.p: Re: Creating a national holiday on Martin Luther King's Birthday. Rudin # P-30

did.unittitle: Carter, JimmyOffice held: As Ex PresidentAddressee: Larry Ervin ClevengerWriter's location: Plains, GA ?

scopecontent.p: Will not run for President in 1992. Will dedicate himself to humane issues and The Carter Center. Rudin # P-31

did.unittitle: Cleveland, GroverOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: John E. DevelinWriter's location: "Executive Mansion Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: The disappointments, pressures and frustrations of being a new President. Rudin # P-32

did.unittitle: Cleveland, GroverOffice held: Period between his two PresidenciesAddressee: Don M. Dickinson, Former Postmaster GeneralWriter's location: New York

scopecontent.p: Though pleased by "the cheers of the people for me...I am enjoying my release from official care more than I can tell you". Rudin # P-33

did.unittitle: Cleveland, GroverOffice held: Period between his two PresidenciesAddressee: Henry WattersonWriter's location: New York

scopecontent.p: His feelings about having been President and what the future of the Party may be. Includes a large etching.1 Rudin # P-34

did.unittitle: Cleveland, GroverOffice held: Period between his two PresidenciesAddressee: Charles Krichbaum, President Young Men's Democratic Club of Canton, Ohio Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.p: Government must merit the people's trust. (With an unrelated letter to Krichbaum from Tom L. Johnson). Rudin # P-35

did.unittitle: Cleveland, GroverOffice held: As President (Second Term)Addressee: Reverend William Neal Cleveland (His brother)Writer's location: "Executive Mansion Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Discusses how he might help financially in a difficult family situation. Rudin # P-36

did.unittitle: Clinton, WilliamOffice held: As Governor of ArkansasAddressee: Jim CoishWriter's location: Little Rock, AK

scopecontent.p: Prepares for a possible 1988 Presidential run. Rudin # P-37

did.unittitle: Clinton, WilliamOffice held: As Governor of ArkansasAddressee: "Sandra Adair Vaughn, Virginia Board of Education"Writer's location: Little Rock, AK

scopecontent.p: Urges the hiring of black teachers. Rudin # P-38

did.unittitle: Clinton, WilliamOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "William H. Natcher, Congressman - Chairman of the Comm. on Appropriations"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Promoting foreign policy measures in support of a democratic Russia. Rudin # P-39

did.unittitle: Clinton, WilliamOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Senate Pass for Impeachment Proceedings. Rudin # P-40
New York Times coverage of Clinton acquittal.

did.unittitle: Coolidge, CalvinOffice held: As Governor of MassachusettsWriter's location: "Executive Chamber Boston, MA"

scopecontent.p: Response to the Boston Police Strike. Rudin # P-41

did.unittitle: Coolidge, CalvinOffice held: As Governor of MassachusettsAddressee: "Wendell P. Stafford, Justice - Supreme Court, District of Columbia"Writer's location: State House, Boston

scopecontent.p: The law and his handling of strikes. "The real remedy for strikes will be the conviction that they do not pay". Rudin # P-42

did.unittitle: Coolidge, CalvinOffice held: As the new PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: His first official proclamation as President declaring a day of mourning for Harding. From the Forbes Collection - Part 2. Rudin # P-43

did.unittitle: Coolidge, CalvinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Delegates of the Boy Scouts of AmericaWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Speech discussing the three fundamentals of Scouting. Rudin # P-44

did.unittitle: Coolidge, CalvinOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Presidential Pay Check. Rudin # P-45

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As Commander-in-ChiefAddressee: Mamie EisenhowerWriter's location: Algiers

scopecontent.p: Loving letter to his wife, Mamie. Rudin # P-46

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As Supreme NATO CommanderAddressee: "Clifford R. Hope, Congressman"Writer's location: Supreme Hdqtrs Allied Powers, Europe

scopecontent.p: Considerations for the 1952 Presidential campaign. From the Forbes Collection - Part 4. Rudin # P-47

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: "Robert P. Skinner, Ambassador (Retired)"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Discusses activities of Voice of America abroad. Rudin # P-48

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: Francis Case, SenatorWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Regarding government spending. Rudin # P-49

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: Richard L. TobinWriter's location: Gettysburg, PA

scopecontent.p: Eisenhower speaks out on the powers of the presidency. With original envelope. Both letter and envelope are marked PERSONAL. Rudin # P-49

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Earl MazoWriter's location: Gettysburg, PA

scopecontent.p: "Comments on the Bay of Pigs, Guatemala and his view of the presidency." Included is Mazo's TLS to Eisenhower dated 8/25/1965. Rudin # P-51

did.unittitle: Eisenhower, Dwight D.Office held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: "Tom H. Parks"Writer's location: Gettysburg, PA

scopecontent.p: "A discussion of our national morals." With Free Franked Envelope. Rudin # P-52

did.unittitle: Fillmore, MillardOffice held: As Comptroller of New York StateAddressee: Hiram C. DayWriter's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.p: Having just heard of his nomination for Vice President on the 1848 ticket of Taylor/Fillmore. Included is a one page letter dated 5/8/1890 from Hiram Day sending this letter to a Rev. Charles C. Pierce who had requested a Fillmore autograph. Rudin # P-53

did.unittitle: Fillmore, MillardOffice held: As Whig candidate for Vice PresidentAddressee: Joshua R. GiddingsWriter's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.p: Concedes the right of the public to examine and criticize his past political conduct. Rudin # P-54

did.unittitle: Fillmore, MillardOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Lydia H. Sigourney, PoetWriter's location: Buffalo, NY

scopecontent.p: Free Franked Envelope. Rudin # P-55

did.unittitle: Fillmore, MillardOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Baltimore political committeeWriter's location: Buffalo, NY

scopecontent.p: Gives his reasons for not speaking at a rally for Bell & Everett but he will give them his vote. Rudin # P-56
Ford memorial envelopes, sheet of 41-cent stamps, first day of issue envelope with 41-cent Ford stamp.

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Thomas E. GoertnerWriter's location: Congress, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: "Concerning Communist teaching in New York schools." With Free Franked Envelope. Rudin # P-57

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Ed HornburgWriter's location: Congress, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: On impeaching Justice Douglas, postal rates, space exploration, and corruption among high government officials ("even high officers...are subject to prosecution when they violate their trust"). Rudin # P-58

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Ms. Joyce VeneklaseWriter's location: Congress, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Comments on abortion. Rudin # P-59

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Press ReleaseWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: S.S. Mayaguez Affair. Rudin # P-60

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Henry Bellmon, SenatorWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Outlines his economic policies and his budget proposals. Rudin # P-61
"A Time To Heal" Harper and Row, New York, NY, Signed copy. See separated material below. Rudin # P-62

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Neil Bogart

scopecontent.p: Discusses the possibility of a candidacy for President. Rudin # P-63

did.unittitle: Ford, GeraldOffice held: As ex-PresidentWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Warren Commission conclusions. Rudin # P-64
The New York Times reporting Ford's death.

did.unittitle: Garfield, JamesOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Mr. E.B. TaylorWriter's location: Washington. D.C.

scopecontent.p: Expresses sympathy for a friend's financial difficulties and offers a loan. Talks about the loss of his child. Tells of his first term in Congress. Rudin # P-65

did.unittitle: Garfield, JamesOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Mr. A.B. Newburgh, Secy Bd of Public Works, Columbus, OhioWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: He is grateful to receive examples of his father's handwriting. Rudin # P-66

did.unittitle: Garfield, JamesOffice held: As U.S. Representative from OhioAddressee: Mrs. John A. DahlgrenWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Assails the influence of money in politics. Rudin # P-67

did.unittitle: Garfield, JamesOffice held: As U.S. Senator and Presidential CandidateAddressee: "John D. Long, Governor of Massachusetts"Writer's location: Mentor, Ohio

scopecontent.p: "The nomination was unsought and unexpected". Rudin # P-68

did.unittitle: Garfield, JamesOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Long Branch

scopecontent.p: "President Garfield Dead" Broadside. Rudin # P-69

did.unittitle: Grant, Ulysses S.Office held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Convenes the Congress to consider pressing matters. Original proclamation. Rudin # P-70

did.unittitle: Grant, Ulysses S.Office held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: H.A. Mumard?Writer's location: Galina, IL

scopecontent.p: Calls for universal suffrage. Rudin # P-71

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: No elective office heldAddressee:

scopecontent.p: Handwritten speech manuscript about the ethics of bankers and the need for regulations.

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: No elective office heldAddressee: Mrs. J.F. DunbaughWriter's location: Frankfurt, Germany

scopecontent.p: Humorous comment on his European church visits. Rudin # P-72

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: As U.S. SenatorAddressee: Howard N. BibleWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Discusses Presidential war authority. Rudin # P-73

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: As Presidential candidateAddressee: Dr. H.M. DunlapWriter's location: Marion, Ohio

scopecontent.p: Maps out the "front porch" strategy for his 1920 Presidential run. From the Forbes Collection Part 4. Rudin # P-74

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: As President Addressee: Mrs. Harriet Taylor Upton, Republican National CommitteeWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Harding is politely chauvinistic in considering a woman to head the Dept. of Welfare. Rudin # P-75

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: PresidentAddressee: Harry Wilson WalkerWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Defends his choice of Harry New as Postmaster General. Rudin # P-76

did.unittitle: Harding, WarrenOffice held: As deceased PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Navy Department General Orders No. 111 announcing the death of Harding and the appointment of Calvin Coolidge as resident. Rudin # P-77

did.unittitle: Harrison, BenjaminOffice held: President-ElectAddressee: Senator John ShermanWriter's location: Indianapolis, IN

scopecontent.p: Harrison struggles to fill his Cabinet posts with the right men. Included is a TLS in answer from Senator Sherman. Rudin # P-78

did.unittitle: Harrison, BenjaminOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Gov. McKinley and Gentlemen of the CommitteeWriter's location: Washington or Indianapolis?

scopecontent.p: Incumbent Harrison accepts the Republican Presidential nomination. From the Forbes Collection Part 3. Rudin # P-79

did.unittitle: Harrison, BenjaminOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Delineation of U.S. boundary with Canada. Rudin # P-80

did.unittitle: Harrison, William H.Office held: As Governor of Indiana TerritoryAddressee: Col. James HenryWriter's location: Vincennes

scopecontent.p: Harrison prepares to acquire massive land holdings in Indiana Territory. Rudin # P-81

did.unittitle: Harrison, William H.Office held: As Senator - Chairman of Senate Committee on Military AffairsAddressee: James Barbour, Secy of WarWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Concerning unclaimed bounty lands due officers and soldiers of the Revolutionary War. Rudin # P-82

did.unittitle: Hayes, Rutherford B.Office held: As Presidential candidateWriter's location: House of Representatives

scopecontent.p: Counting of the vote for President and Vice President. Rudin # P-83

did.unittitle: Hayes, Rutherford B.Office held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Presidential pardon for a man convicted of manslaughter. Countersigned by William M. Everts, Secretary of State. Rudin # P-84

did.unittitle: Hayes, Rutherford B.Office held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: William ClarkWriter's location: Fremont, Ohio

scopecontent.p: Discusses his activities during his retirement. Rudin # P-85

did.unittitle: Hoover, HerbertOffice held: As Chairman of American Relief AdministrationAddressee: Kent Perkins, Director of Publicity for Mass.Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.p: Hoover says it is an honor to be thought of as a possible President. Outlines his political philosophy. Rudin # P-86

did.unittitle: Hoover, HerbertOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Dr. Richard L. Weil, University of Wisconsin"Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Presidential Proclamation of Thanksgiving Day. Also included is a Hoover medallion. Rudin # P-87

did.unittitle: Hoover, HerbertOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Proclamation announcing the death of Calvin Coolidge. Countersigned by Henry Stimson, Secretary of State. Rudin # P-88

did.unittitle: Hoover, HerbertOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Henry L. StoddardWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Reacts eight weeks after surrendering The White House to FDR. Rudin # P-89

did.unittitle: Hoover, HerbertOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: "Wheeler McMillen,Editor of The Farm Journal"Writer's location: The Waldorf Astoria, NY

scopecontent.p: States his views in support of a food administrator during WWII, preferably the Secretary of Agriculture. Includes Hoover cover letter, Hoover statement, and statement, "The Importance of Food in this War" from H.B. Frese to Mr. Pate. Rudin # P-90

did.unittitle: Hoover, HerbertOffice held: As ex-PresidentWriter's location: The Waldorf Astoria, NY

scopecontent.p: Reading copy of speech given when receiving the Moles Award. "The Moles 10th Annual Award Dinner". Rudin # P-91

did.unittitle: Jackson, AndrewOffice held: As Major GeneralAddressee: Samuel JusticeWriter's location: Fort Jackson

scopecontent.p: Remitting the death sentence on a deserter. Rudin # P-92

did.unittitle: Jackson, AndrewOffice held: As Major GeneralAddressee: Samuel CarswellWriter's location: Nashville, TN

scopecontent.p: Regarding his nephew's admission to West Point. Rudin # P-92A
Two commemorative envelopes with 6 first day of issue 1-cent stamps, one commemorative envelope with four 2-cent stamps, one envelope with four 13-cent stamps.

did.unittitle: Jefferson, ThomasOffice held: As Secretary of StateWriter's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.p: Act of Congress pertaining to the procuring of seals for the Court system. Rudin # P-93

did.unittitle: Johnson, AndrewOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Commissioning Edward D. Townsend as Brevet Major General. Rudin # P-94

did.unittitle: Johnson, AndrewOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Impeachment Ticket. Rudin # P-95
New York Times paper on impeachment acquittal.

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As Senate Majority LeaderAddressee: "Robert K. Pepper - News Editor, Fort Meyers News Press"Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Discusses how to handle Russia. See Nixon letter of 5/14/57 also written to Pepper. Rudin # P-96

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As SenatorAddressee: H. Lane ChandlerWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Explains what America means to him. Rudin # P-97

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "James Roosevelt, House of Representatives"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: The issue of equal rights for African Americans. Rudin # P-98

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As Senate Democratic Leader Addressee: James RooseveltWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Grateful for your warm message. (Johnson had just won the Vice Presidency under Kennedy). Rudin # P-101

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: James RooseveltWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Birthday Congratulations to Roosevelt. Rudin # P-100

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: James Roosevelt Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Election campaign letter. (Johnson is running for President). Rudin # P-99

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: James RooseveltWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Thanks Roosevelt for his birthday greeting. Rudin # P-102

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Eugene G. Mattison Jr., Administrative Director, Plans for Progress"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Discusses "providing equal opportunity for all our citizens". Rudin # P-103

did.unittitle: Johnson, LyndonOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Cornelius Vanderbilt Jr.Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Regarding nomination of Thurgood Marshall to the Supreme Court. Rudin # P-104
JFK "Life in Stamps" envelopes and stamps, photograph with 5 cent stamp, inauguration envelope and stamp.

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F.Office held: As SenatorAddressee: Mr. C.P. CronkWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Concerning the Bricker Resolution and congressional salaries. Rudin # P-105

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F.Office held: As SenatorAddressee: "Mrs. A.E. Cassidy, Chairman Mass. State Fed. of Women's Clubs"Writer's location: "U.S. Senate Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: "I most emphatically favor non-discriminatory treatment of women". Rudin # P-105A

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F.Office held: As SenatorAddressee: Arthur Krock, N.Y. Times and William E. DuganWriter's location: "U.S. Senate Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Discussing his stands and those of his father. See also Bishop Fulton Sheen letter to William E. Dugan in collection #6875. Rudin # P-105B

did.unittitle: "Kennedy, John F. (Janet Travell, M.D.)"Office held: As SenatorAddressee: Dr. Janet Travell to Robert F. KennedyWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Re: John F. Kennedy's health. Rudin # P-106

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F.Office held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: President Kennedy's schedule of appointments during his first day in office. Rudin # P-107

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F. (Janet Travell, M.D.)Office held: As PresidentAddressee: "Leonard Lyons, New York Post"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Reacting to the market for his signature. Rudin # P-108

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F.Office held: As PresidentAddressee: Speaker of the House of RepresentativesWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Amendment to the budget (for foreign assistance) for fiscal year 1964. Rudin # P-109

did.physdesc: 9 sheets of various lengths, approx 8 1/2" wide by approx 99" in total length66 apx. 8 1/2" x 11" photos

scopecontent.p: Original Associated Press Teletypes. Mainly of the events of Nov. 24, 1963. Photos show events from Nov. 22 to Nov. 25, 1963. Rudin # P-110
Original Associated Press Teletypes. Mainly of the events of Nov. 22 (Friday), Nov. 23 (Saturday) and Nov. 25 (Monday), 1963. Rudin # P-111
Original Teletype rolls, regarding the assassination. Rudin # P-112
Original UPI/AP Teletypes. Received on teletype machines of the Santa Barbara News-Press. Together with two original AP wire photos as received by the Santa Barbara News-Press. Rudin # P-113
Original AP Teletypes regarding the assassination. Rudin # P-114
Original AP Teletypes regarding the assassination. Rudin # P-115

did.physdesc: 8 rolls, apx. 100 ft +, 12 individual "tear off" sheets -

scopecontent.p: Original UPI Teletypes, the entire day of the assassination is chronicled. Rudin # P-116
6 copies of the New York Times and one copy of the Dallas Times Herald covering the assassination.

did.unittitle: Madison, JamesOffice held: As Secretary of StateAddressee: Governor of MassachusettsWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Announcement of the passage of the Twelfth Amendment to the Constitution. Rudin # P-117

scopecontent.p: Patent for an early form of carbon paper. Rudin # P-118

did.unittitle: Madison, JamesOffice held: As President Also signed by James Monroe, Secy of StateAddressee: Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Ship's passport for the Brig Calypso of Boston. See also Tyler for Sea Letter. Rudin # P-119

did.unittitle: McKinley, WilliamOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Mr. J.N. TaylorWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Writes concerning the upcoming campaign. Rudin # P-120

did.unittitle: McKinley, WilliamOffice held: As Governor of OhioAddressee: "John D. Long, Governor of Massachusetts"Writer's location: Canton, Ohio

scopecontent.p: McKinley working on forming his Cabinet almost ten months before being elected. Rudin # P-121

did.unittitle: McKinley, WilliamOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Honorable C.K. Davis, U.S. Senator"Writer's location: "Executive Mansion Washington, D.C. "

scopecontent.p: Soliciting Davis to be a member of Paris Peace Commission to end Spanish American War. Rudin # P-122
New York Tribune and The Franklin Sentinel coverage of assassination of McKinley.
The Sun coverage of McKinley's death.

did.unittitle: Monroe, JamesOffice held: As Secy of StateWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Privateer Agreement for the ship "Union of Philadelphia". Rudin # P-123

did.unittitle: Monroe, JamesOffice held: As Secy of StateWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Additional instructions to the public and private armed vessels of the United States. Rudin # P-124

did.unittitle: Monroe, JamesOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: John GatesWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Land grant rewarded to Private John Gates. Rudin # P-125
Commemorative envelopes with 5-cent stamps and 3-cent stamps.
Commemorative and memorial envelopes and stamps.

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As Lieutenant Commander, U.S. NavyAddressee: "Roy O. Day - Chairman 12th Congressional District"Writer's location: Middle River, Maryland

scopecontent.p: Prepares for his run for public office. Rudin # P-126

scopecontent.p: Discusses how to handle Russia. See Lyndon Johnson letter of 7/22/57, also written to Mr. Pepper. Rudin # P-127

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As candidate for Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: Congressman Bob WilsonWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Proper governance demands "political leaders of absolute probity and judgement". Rudin # P-128

scopecontent.p: Discusses the office of the Vice Presidency. Rudin # P-129

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As President?Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Inauguration Envelope. Rudin # P-130

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As President Addressee: Senator Jennings RandolphWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Regarding Carswell appointment to the Supreme Court. Rudin # P-131

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Official printed ticket to impeachment. Rudin # P-132

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Congressman Albert H. QuieWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Regarding resigning the Presidency. Rudin # P-133

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As Presidential candidateWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Announcing Nixon's resignation and Ford's ascension to office. Rudin # P-134
The New York Times coverage of Nixon's resignation.

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As ex-PresidentWriter's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.p: Contains commentary about the media and "beating them".

did.unittitle: Nixon, RichardOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Senator Daniel Patrick MoynihanWriter's location: Woodcliff Lake, NJ

scopecontent.p: Discusses race relations. Rudin # P-138
New York Times coverage of Nixon's death.
Commemorative envelopes with 42-cent stamps, foreign stamps, inaugural covers, and note cards with stamps.

did.unittitle: Obama, BarackOffice held: Addressee: Writer's location: "Crown Publishers New York, NY"

scopecontent.p: "The Audacity of Hope". See separated material below. Rudin # P-138A
The New York Times special publication of inaugurations, from Washington to Obama. Rudin # N-16
Four newspapers/magazines on the election and inauguration. Rudin # N-16
Press plate announcing Obama's win.

did.unittitle: Pierce, FranklinOffice held: As CongressmanAddressee: Pierce's FatherWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Concerning his attention to religious service, public and private. Pierce's letter is a continuation of a letter written by his brother, also to their father. Rudin # P-139

did.unittitle: Pierce, FranklinOffice held: As Brigadier GeneralAddressee: Endorses letter of Lt. Col. Joshua HowardWriter's location: Chapultepec, Mexico

scopecontent.p: Howard's letter recommends promotions and Certificates of Merit for bravery following the Battle of Churubusco and Chapultepec. Rudin # P-140

did.unittitle: Pierce, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Proclamation concerning the boundary between the United States and Mexico. Rudin # P-141

did.unittitle: Polk, JamesOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Orders that the battle flags captured during the Mexican War be deposited at West Point. Rudin # P-142
Commemorative envelopes and stamps.

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: Probably as Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: Tom Reed

scopecontent.p: Thinks U.S. foreign policy should be "a little hard nosed". Rudin # P-143

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: As Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: Sgt. Ernie A. SiegristWriter's location: Sacramento, CA

scopecontent.p: Response to an angry serviceman in Vietnam. Rudin # P-144

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: As Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: George F. BarrettWriter's location: Sacramento, CA

scopecontent.p: Returns a campaign contribution. Rudin # P-145

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: As Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: Mike FitchettWriter's location: Sacramento, CA

scopecontent.p: Comments on the reform of the Electoral College. Rudin # P-146

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: As Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: Mrs. Donald S. BoltonWriter's location: Sacramento, CA

scopecontent.p: Discusses tax relief. Rudin # P-147

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: As ex-Governor of CaliforniaAddressee: Lloyd E. RohrkeWriter's location: Los Angeles, CA

scopecontent.p: Has soured on being a legislator. "anyway it's not for me." Rudin # P-148

did.unittitle: Reagan, RonaldOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: The Honorable and Mrs. James BradyWriter's location: (Likely California)

scopecontent.p: Congratulations on the passage of the Brady Bill. Rudin # P-149
Commemorative envelopes and stamps

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: No public office heldAddressee: Mrs. Teresa GrahamWriter's location: Hyde Park, NY

scopecontent.p: Outlines his plan for strengthening the Democratic Party. Rudin # P-150

scopecontent.p: The definition of ideal citizenship. Rudin # P-151

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As Governor of New YorkAddressee: Mr. H.S. WellmanWriter's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.p: Regarding the Patients Aid Fund at the Georgia Warm Springs Foundation. From the Dr. Joseph Plaud Collection. Rudin # P-152

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As Governor of New YorkAddressee: Walter L. BennettWriter's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.p: Response to a detractor. Rudin # P-152A

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As Governor of New YorkAddressee: Lewis A. LincolnWriter's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.p: I am a Democrat "by conviction". Rudin # P-153

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As Governor of New YorkAddressee: Mr. W.F. BussellWriter's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.p: Regarding the Square Deal, Democratic Principles, and the suffering American people. Rudin # P-154

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: President

scopecontent.p: Program for the Inaugural Ceremonies.

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "George Creel, Colliers Magazine"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: An early trial balloon in FDR's battle against the Supreme Court. See also FDR letter of 12/18/36. Rudin # P-155

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Basil O'ConnorWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: "The national fight against Infantile Paralysis cannot cease. It must go on!" Rudin # P-156

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Samuel I. Rosenman, Roosevelt's Speech Writer"Writer's location: On U.S.S. Indianapolis

scopecontent.p: FDR misses Rosenman's speech writing expertise. Rudin # P-157

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Basil O'ConnorWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Prepares to manipulate the press. See also FDR letter of 9/19/35. Rudin # P-158

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Norman Thomas, Socialist Party Leader"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Regarding the export of arms to Spain during the Spanish Civil War. Rudin # P-159

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: William S. Paley, President, Columbia Broadcasting SystemWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: FDR praises Paley for keeping the radio airwaves open for public and political discussions. Rudin # P-160

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Gutzon BorglumWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Roosevelt writes to Gutzon Borglum, sculptor of Mount Rushmore, concerning the Monument's creation and funding. Rudin # P-160AA

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Heads of All Departments and Independent EstablishmentsWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Directive relating to the employment of negroes in the Federal Civil Service. Rudin # P-160A

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Martin C. SweeneyWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: FDR sends sly thanks for a gift of liquor. Rudin # P-161

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Dr. Harry L. HopkinsWriter's location: Warm Springs, GA

scopecontent.p: FDR pokes fun at Thomas Dewey's mustache. From the Dr. Joseph Plaud Collection. Rudin # P-162

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Edwin B. WilsonWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Eight days prior to his death, Roosevelt sends greetings to All Souls Universalist Church, Brooklyn. He then states, "not one of the least of our cherished freedoms is freedom of conscience. It is a cornerstone of our national happiness -- a freedom which we are now defending and shall always defend as a bullwork of democracy and civilization." Also includes letter from Matthew Connelly to Cornelius Greenway, Acknowledgement of Greenway's letter of April 21, 1945, probably requesting a message from the President, which had already been sent before Roosevelt's death (dated April 27, 1945). Rudin # P-163

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, FranklinOffice held: As deceased PresidentWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Official naval teletype announcing death of FDR. Rudin # P-164
Philadelphia Inquirer coverage of Roosevelt's death and Truman's oath.
Commemorative stamps and envelopes.

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, TheodoreOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Honorable Morris K. Jessup, President, Chamber of Commerce State of New York"Writer's location: "Executive Mansion Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Declines to speak at the Chamber of Commerce "until my message goes before Congress". Rudin # P-165

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, TheodoreOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: "Mr. O.K. Davis, New York Times"Writer's location: """The Outlook"" New York, NY"

scopecontent.p: "No situation could arise which would make it possible for me to accept a nomination next year". See also letter of 12/14/1911 - from the Forbes Collection - Part 6. Rudin # P-166

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, TheodoreOffice held: As ex-PresidentWriter's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.p: Made out to Harvard Alumni Association. Rudin # P-167

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, TheodoreOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: J.M. HummelWriter's location: """The Outlook"" New York, NY"

scopecontent.p: "I do not want to be nominated for any office". See also letter of 5/31/1911. Rudin # P-168

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, TheodoreOffice held: As ex-President about to be nominated by the new Progressive PartyAddressee: Lady GregoryWriter's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.p: Discusses his feelings as Progressive candidate in the 1912 presidential election. Included is a TLS (secretarially signed) dated 10/19/1912. From the hospital, Roosevelt mentions campaigning and the unsuccessful assassination attempt on his life. Rudin # P-169

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, TheodoreOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: "Dr. Henry Waldo Coe, Senator from Oregon"Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.p: "The public at large had gotten tired of all of us reformers and especially of me". Rudin # P-170

did.unittitle: Taft, William H.Office held: As PresidentWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Archive consisting of two drafts of Taft's "Special Message of the President of the United States, Returning Without Approval House Bill No. 12812. An Act to reduce the duties on Manufacture of Cotton". Rudin # P-171

did.unittitle: Taft, William H. Addressee: Walter SmithWriter's location: Washington, DC.

scopecontent.p: Taft discusses his loss to Woodrow Wilson in the 1912 election.

did.unittitle: Taft, William H.Office held: "As ex-President and Professor of Law, Yale University"Addressee: S.G. BayneWriter's location: New Haven, CT

scopecontent.p: Discusses losing some of his weight. Rudin # P-172

did.unittitle: Taft, William H.Office held: As ex-President and Joint Chairman of the National War Labor BoardAddressee: "Clarence H. Kelsey, President, Title Guarantee and Trust Co."Writer's location: Pointe-au-Pic, Quebec, Canada

scopecontent.p: Sees a long war continuing, discusses Russia and Germany, calls for a larger American draft. Rudin # P-173

did.unittitle: Taft, William H.Office held: "As ex-President and Professor of Law, Yale University"Addressee: "Dudley E. Waters, President Grand Rapids Bank"Writer's location: New Haven, CT

scopecontent.p: Discusses sound education and the formation of educational funds. Rudin # P-174

did.unittitle: Taft, William H.Office held: As ex-President and Chief Justice of the United StatesAddressee: "Clarence H. Kelsey, President, Title Guarantee and Trust Co."Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Regarding his High Court colleagues. Rudin # P-175

did.unittitle: Taft, William H.Office held: As ex-President and Chief Justice of the United StatesAddressee: Honorable Charles G. WashburnWriter's location: Pointe-au-Pic Quebec, Canada

scopecontent.p: Acknowledges his 70th birthday; discusses what he would like to do in his remaining years. Rudin # P-176

did.unittitle: Taylor, ZacharyOffice held: As Major GeneralWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Part of a Pay Voucher including servants.

processnote.p: Moved to the Gail and Stephen Rudin Slavery Collection, collection #4681. Rudin # P-177

did.unittitle: Taylor, ZacharyOffice held: As Major GeneralAddressee: Mr. S.C. HoldenWriter's location: Camp near Monterray, Mexico

scopecontent.p: Citizens of Batavia, NY endorse Taylor for President. He says that there are much better men than himself who are qualified. With newspaper article. Rudin # P-178
Envelope with stamps.

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Senator Walter F. GeorgeWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Reviews troop redeployment at war's end. Rudin # P-179
Chicago Daily Tribune of the election.

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: David H. MorganWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Ridicules the hypocritical Republican platform. Rudin # P-180

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: "Cabinet Room The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Authorizing loan for construction of U.N. Headquarters in N.Y. City. Rudin # P-181

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Wilbur J. AbellWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: "We have tried to arrange things so that (Margaret) would attain that success on merit". Rudin # P-182

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Barney Nover The Denver Post"Writer's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Refers to his attempted assassination. Rudin # P-183

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: Edward D. McKimWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: On the firing of MacArthur. From the Forbes Collection - Part Six. Rudin # P-184

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Paul V. LutzWriter's location: Independence, Missouri

scopecontent.p: Former Presidents are discarded. Rudin # P-185

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Winston NormanWriter's location: Independence, Missouri

scopecontent.p: Says "No" to running for President in 1960. Included is a copy of a letter from Norman to Truman (1/8/1950) asking him to accept the Presidential nomination. Envelope of Truman letter has a printed Free Frank signature. Rudin # P-186

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Honorable Leonor K. SullivanWriter's location: Independence, Missouri

scopecontent.p: Vigorously campaigns for the Democratic Presidential ticket of John Kennedy and Lyndon Johnson. Rudin # P-187

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Edmund G. Brown, Governor, CaliforniaWriter's location: Independence, Missouri

scopecontent.p: Truman comments on the political wisdom of the American people. Also includes unsigned copy of another letter to Truman from Brown, "I lost, but your letter was of tremendous help. I should have had the sense you had and not run for a third term"... Rudin # P-188

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Robert LovettWriter's location: Independence, Missouri

scopecontent.p: How a teacher should communicate with students. Envelope has printed Free Frank Signature. Rudin # P-189

did.unittitle: Truman, HarryOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Miss. Maria SmithWriter's location: Independence, Missouri

scopecontent.p: Explains the "S" in his name. Envelope has printed Free Frank Signature. Rudin # P-190

did.unittitle: Tyler, JohnOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location:

scopecontent.p: Four Language Sea Letter for the Bark "Elizabeth" for a whaling voyage. Rudin # P-191

did.unittitle: Tyler, JohnOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Robert Tyler (his son)Writer's location: Sherwood Forest

scopecontent.p: Mourns the loss of his grandson to cholera. Rudin # P-192

did.unittitle: Van Buren, MartinOffice held: As Secretary of StateWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Certificate of Copyright for the book "Sketches of Naval Life". Rudin # P-193

did.unittitle: Van Buren, MartinOffice held: As Vice PresidentAddressee: William C. RivesWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: Reports on diplomatic affairs involving France and Great Britain. Rudin # P-194

did.unittitle: Van Buren, MartinOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: Henry D. GilpinWriter's location: Lindenwald

scopecontent.p: Political letter on the 1856 Presidential Election, Van Buren's relationship with James Buchanan, and the Democratic Party's chances in New York State. Rudin # P-195
Commemorative stamps.

did.unittitle: Washington, GeorgeOffice held: As ex-Commander of the Continental ArmyAddressee: Colonel John FitzpatrickWriter's location: Mount Vernon

scopecontent.p: The toil of a legislator re: the Potomac Company. Not in "Writings" ed. J.C. Fitzpatrick. Rudin # P-196

did.unittitle: Wilson, WoodrowOffice held: "As Professor of Law and Politics, Princeton University"Addressee: Frank Walter BrooksWriter's location: "Judd Haven, Ontario, Canada"

scopecontent.p: On the Constitution as a living document. Included with this letter is correspondence regarding its possible sale to Thomas Madigan in 1936. Madigan was a well known autograph dealer of his day who wrote a book titled "Word Shadows of the Great". Rudin # P-197

did.unittitle: Wilson, WoodrowOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: In the wake of Pershing's pursuit of Pancho Villa, Wilson tries to manage the press. From the Forbes Collection - Part One. Rudin # P-198

did.unittitle: Wilson, WoodrowOffice held: As PresidentWriter's location: "The White House Washington, D.C."

scopecontent.p: Vote of the Immigration Bill. H.R. 10384. Rudin # P-199

did.unittitle: Wilson, WoodrowOffice held: As PresidentAddressee: "Clarence Dillon, War Industries Board "Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: First "dollar a year men" check. Countersigned by Bernard Baruch. Rudin # P-200

did.unittitle: Wilson, WoodrowOffice held: As ex-PresidentAddressee: "Eleanor Wilson McAdoo (Wilson's Daughter)"Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.p: "In spite of breakdown and political persecution, I realize that I am the most fortunate man in the world". Rudin # P-201

list.head: Signed Books
list.item: Keeping Faith: Memoirs of a President, by Jimmy Carter, 1982.
A Time to Heal, by Gerald Ford, 1979.
No More Vietnams, by Richard Nixon. 2 copies, one unsigned.
The Audacity of Hope, by Barack Obama.
Report of the Commissioner of Patents for the year 1849, Office of Printers to the Senate. Contains patent listing for Abraham Lincoln.

odd.p: Other Presidential letters can be found in the Frank Thilly Papers, #14-21-623, Wilson, Taft; Johnson family papers, #4928, Adams; Jared Sparks Collection of Autographs, #4600 Bd. Ms. 548 ++, Washington.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Gail and Stephen Rudin American presidential collection, #6844. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
